description: Fix two bugs in the plugin SDK: a runtime crash when logging SVG DOM elements (className is SVGAnimatedString, not string), and getPageGlobal failing to traverse through function-typed intermediate values.

userStories.title: Fix safeSerializeArg crash on SVG DOM elements
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want logging SVG DOM elements to produce a safe string representation instead of throwing a TypeError so that the safe serializer in the SDK log infrastructure actually lives up to its name.
acceptanceCriteria: safeSerializeArg handles SVG elements where node.className is an SVGAnimatedString (not a string)
For SVG elements, the class name is obtained via node.getAttribute('class') or a typeof guard
Logging an SVG element produces a string like '[SVG#myId.myClass]' or '[SVG]' — not a crash
Logging regular HTML elements still works as before
Existing unit tests pass; new test case covers SVG element serialization
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-sdk/src/log.ts, around line 46-50.

The bug: Line 48 does `node.className.split(' ')`. For SVG elements (`<svg>`, `<rect>`, `<path>`, etc.), `className` is an `SVGAnimatedString` object, not a string. `SVGAnimatedString` has no `.split()` method, so this throws `TypeError: node.className.split is not a function`.

This is especially bad because `safeSerializeArg` is the 'safe' serializer — it should NEVER throw. If a plugin does `log.info('Found element', svgNode)`, the entire log call crashes.

Fix: Replace line 48 with a typeof guard:
```typescript
const cls = typeof node.className === 'string' ? node.className : (node as Element).getAttribute?.('class') ?? '';
const className = cls ? `.${cls.split(' ')[0] ?? ''}` : '';
```

Check platform/plugin-sdk/src/log.test.ts for existing tests. The test environment is jsdom which may or may not support SVGAnimatedString. If not available in jsdom, mock the node with `{ nodeType: 1, nodeName: 'SVG', className: { baseVal: 'myClass' } }` — the key thing to test is that a non-string className doesn't crash.

userStories.title: Fix getPageGlobal to traverse function-typed values
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want getPageGlobal('SomeLib.config.token') to work when SomeLib is a function/constructor with properties so that I can read globals from function-based namespaces.
acceptanceCriteria: getPageGlobal allows traversal through values where typeof === 'function' (in addition to 'object')
getPageGlobal('SomeConstructor.staticProp') returns the static property value when SomeConstructor is a function
getPageGlobal still returns undefined for null and undefined intermediate values
getPageGlobal still returns undefined for primitive intermediates (string, number, boolean)
Existing unit tests pass; new test case covers function-typed traversal
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-sdk/src/page-state.ts, line 17.

The bug: The guard `typeof current !== 'object'` on line 17 rejects function-typed values. In JavaScript, functions can have properties (e.g., `MyLib.config.token` where `MyLib` is a function). `typeof function === 'function'`, not `'object'`, so the traversal returns undefined when hitting a function in the path.

Fix: Change line 17 from:
```typescript
if (current === null || current === undefined || typeof current !== 'object') return undefined;
```
to:
```typescript
if (current === null || current === undefined) return undefined;
if (typeof current !== 'object' && typeof current !== 'function') return undefined;
```

Check platform/plugin-sdk/src/page-state.test.ts for existing tests.
